passage:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
- Natural language processing (NLP) is a field of artificial intelligence focused on enabling computers to understand and generate human language.
- Early NLP systems relied on hand-coded grammatical rules and struggled with ambiguity and context.
- Modern NLP systems use large language models (LLMs) trained on vast amounts of text to predict likely word sequences.
- LLMs have achieved near-human performance on several language benchmarks, including reading comprehension and question answering.
- Critics note that LLMs sometimes generate confident but factually incorrect responses, a phenomenon called 'hallucination.'

choices.A: While early NLP systems relied on hand-coded rules and struggled with ambiguity, modern large language models trained on vast text datasets have reached near-human performance on language benchmarks—though they can also generate confident but incorrect responses.
choices.B: Natural language processing is a field of AI focused on enabling computers to understand and generate human language.
choices.C: Large language models have achieved near-human performance on reading comprehension and question answering benchmarks.
choices.D: LLMs sometimes produce 'hallucinations'—confident but factually incorrect responses.

explanation: The goal is to compare early and modern NLP and note a key limitation of the modern approach. A contrasts the two approaches (hand-coded rules and ambiguity problems vs. data-trained LLMs and near-human performance) and ends with the hallucination limitation. It accomplishes all three elements: comparison + limitation. B defines NLP broadly without comparing or noting a limitation. C reports only modern LLM success without early NLP comparison or limitation. D presents only the hallucination problem without the comparison.
question: The student wants to compare early NLP systems with modern ones and note a key limitation of the modern approach. Which choice most effectively uses the notes to accomplish this goal?
